story.iWant: to update thread endpoints for ownership and branching support
story.soThat: threads can support personal and project types with proper authorization
story.tasks: - Add type, owner_id, project_id, created_by fields to ThreadDTO and ThreadEntity (AC: #1) - Update GET /api/threads endpoint with type and projectId filters (AC: #2, #3, #4, #9) - Update POST /api/threads endpoint for project thread creation (AC: #5, #6) - Implement authorization checks using AuthorizationService (AC: #7, #8) - Update PATCH /api/threads/{id} endpoint for new fields (AC: #1) - Add backward compatibility testing (AC: #1, #5, #6)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: ThreadDTO includes new fields: type, ownerId, projectId, createdBy (per API §3.1)
GET /api/threads?type=personal returns only personal threads (project_id IS NULL)
GET /api/threads?type=project returns only project threads (project_id IS NOT NULL)
GET /api/threads?projectId={uuid} returns threads for specific project only
POST /api/threads with projectId creates project thread (type='project', project_id set)
POST /api/threads without projectId creates personal thread (type='personal', project_id NULL)
Personal threads accessible only by owner (user_id matches JWT sub)
Project threads require project membership (AuthorizationService enforces view role minimum)
Thread list pagination works correctly (page, size parameters, totalElements count accurate)

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: ThreadController handles Thread CRUD endpoints, filtering, pagination. ThreadService handles business logic, authorization checks. ThreadRepository handles database queries, filtering by type/project.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: GET /api/threads?type={personal|project|all}&projectId={uuid}&page={int}&size={int} → Response: PagedResponse<ThreadDTO>. POST /api/threads Request: {title?, projectId?, metadata?} → Response: ThreadDTO (201 Created). PATCH /api/threads/{threadId} Request: {title?, status?, metadata?} → Response: ThreadDTO (200 OK).

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: New Phase 2 fields: type VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'personal' ('personal' | 'project'), owner_id UUID NOT NULL, project_id UUID (nullable, FK to projects), created_by UUID NOT NULL. CHECK constraints: type IN ('personal', 'project'), (type = 'personal' AND project_id IS NULL) OR (type = 'project' AND project_id IS NOT NULL).

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: requireThreadAccess(UUID threadId, UUID userId) checks thread access (owner for personal, project membership for project threads). requireThreadModify(UUID threadId, UUID userId) checks modify permission (owner for personal, edit+ for project threads).

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: AC#9: ThreadDTO includes new fields. AC#10-#12: Type and projectId filters work. AC#13-#14: Project/personal thread creation works. AC#15-#16: Authorization enforced. AC#17: Pagination works correctly.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: 1. Update ThreadEntity with new columns. 2. Update ThreadDTO to expose new fields. 3. Update ThreadMapper for entity ↔ DTO conversion. 4. Add type/projectId filters to ThreadRepository.findByUser(). 5. Inject AuthorizationService into ThreadController. 6. Add authorization checks before thread operations.

doc.title: System Architecture
doc.snippet: Moku API (Spring Boot) handles authentication, authorization, data persistence, API gateway. Provides REST endpoints for threads, messages, projects, workflows.

artifact.reason: Desktop Thread interface consumed by UI - reference for API DTO structure (currently missing type/ownerId/projectId fields)
Desktop ThreadAPI defines getAll() with projectId filter, create(), update() - shows expected API contract from Desktop perspective
Desktop ThreadRepository interface for local storage - reference for thread data model structure
Desktop Project interface - shows projectId structure expected in Thread references
Existing thread handler unit tests - reference for test patterns (vitest, mocking IPC)
Existing thread management e2e tests - reference for integration test patterns (Playwright)

story-context.constraints: - **Repository Separation:** Moku API backend is SEPARATE repository from this desktop project. ThreadController/Service/Repository implementation is in Moku API codebase, NOT in this desktop project. - **Desktop Role:** This desktop project CONSUMES Moku API endpoints. Desktop changes for this story are minimal (TypeScript interface updates to match new API fields). - **API Layer Standards:** Use Spring Boot REST conventions (@RestController, @GetMapping, @PostMapping, @PatchMapping). All endpoints return standardized JSON responses with consistent error handling (4xx/5xx with error codes). - **Pagination:** Must support limit/cursor for list operations to handle large datasets. - **Authorization:** Authorization checks must execute before any data mutations or sensitive reads. - **Service Layer Patterns:** ThreadService handles business logic (validation, authorization delegation, entity creation). ThreadRepository handles data access (JPA queries, filtering, pagination). AuthorizationService provides centralized permission checks. Use @Transactional annotation on service methods. - **DTO/Entity Mapping:** Use ThreadMapper for entity ↔ DTO conversion. DTOs expose only necessary fields to API consumers. Entities contain JPA annotations and database-specific logic. Never expose entities directly in API responses. - **Authorization Rules:** Personal threads: owner_id must match JWT sub claim. Project threads: User must be project member with at least 'view' role. Edit operations: User must be owner (personal) or have 'edit' role (project). AuthorizationService.requireThreadAccess() throws AccessDeniedException if unauthorized. - **Backward Compatibility:** API changes must be additive only (new fields optional). Phase 1 desktop app must continue working after deployment. New ThreadDTO fields have sensible defaults for existing threads. - **Dependency on E1-S1:** Database schema changes from E1-S1 must be applied first (V2.1 migration). New columns (type, owner_id, project_id, created_by) must exist in database. Verify E1-S1 is complete before starting this story. - **Dependency on E1-S5:** AuthorizationService will be implemented in E1-S5. For this story, create AuthorizationService stub/interface if E1-S5 not complete. Actual authorization logic will be implemented by E1-S5.

interface.name: GET /api/threads
interface.signature: GET /api/threads?type={personal|project|all}&projectId={uuid}&page={int}&size={int} → Response: PagedResponse<ThreadDTO> {content: [{id, title, type, ownerId, projectId, status, createdAt, updatedAt, messageCount, branchCount}], totalElements: int, totalPages: int}

interface.name: GET /api/threads/{threadId}
interface.signature: GET /api/threads/{threadId} → Response: ThreadDetailDTO {id, title, type, ownerId, projectId, createdBy, status, createdAt, updatedAt, metadata, messages: [MessageDTO]}

interface.name: POST /api/threads
interface.signature: POST /api/threads Request: {title?, projectId?, metadata?} → Response: ThreadDTO (201 Created)

interface.name: PATCH /api/threads/{threadId}
interface.signature: PATCH /api/threads/{threadId} Request: {title?, status?, metadata?} → Response: ThreadDTO (200 OK). NOTE: type, projectId, ownerId, createdBy are immutable after creation (return 400 if client attempts to change)

interface.name: AuthorizationService.requireThreadAccess()
interface.signature: public void requireThreadAccess(UUID threadId, UUID userId) throws AccessDeniedException - Checks thread access (owner for personal, project membership for project threads)

interface.name: AuthorizationService.requireThreadModify()
interface.signature: public void requireThreadModify(UUID threadId, UUID userId) throws AccessDeniedException - Checks modify permission (owner for personal, edit+ for project threads)

tests.standards: Use JUnit 5 for test structure. Use Mockito 5.x for mocking services/repositories. Use Spring Boot Test with @SpringBootTest for integration tests. Use Testcontainers for PostgreSQL database in integration tests. Test service logic: ThreadService method behavior with mocked repository. Test repository queries: ThreadRepository filter logic with mocked data. Test API endpoints: ThreadController with real HTTP requests (MockMvc or RestAssured). Test authorization: Verify 403 responses for unauthorized access. Test pagination: Create large dataset, verify page/size/totalElements.
tests.locations: moku-api/src/test/java/com/holokai/moku/ (unit and integration tests for Moku API backend)

test.idea: Unit test: ThreadMapperTest verifying new fields (type, ownerId, projectId, createdBy) serialize/deserialize correctly between ThreadEntity and ThreadDTO
Integration test: GET /api/threads?type=personal, create 5 personal + 5 project threads, verify only 5 personal returned
Integration test: GET /api/threads?type=project, verify only project threads returned (project_id IS NOT NULL)
Integration test: GET /api/threads?projectId={uuid}, create threads in project A and B, query for A, verify only A threads returned
Integration test: POST /api/threads with projectId, verify response has type='project', project_id set correctly
Integration test: POST /api/threads without projectId, verify response has type='personal', project_id=NULL
Integration test: Create personal thread as userA, attempt GET as userB, expect 403 Forbidden
Integration test: Create project thread, attempt GET as non-member, expect 403. GET as member with view role, expect 200
Integration test: Create 100 threads, GET /api/threads?page=0&size=50, verify 50 returned, totalElements=100, totalPages=2
Integration test: PATCH /api/threads/{id} with attempt to change type field, expect 400 Bad Request with message 'Field is immutable'
Integration test: Query Phase 1 thread (migrated with V2.1 defaults) after migration, expect 200 with type='personal', project_id=NULL
